name: Blades
description: Dual analog 2-pole (12dB/oct) state-variable filter with CV-morphed LP/BP/HP response, continuously variable single/parallel/series routing, clean self-oscillation as sine VCO, and a per-channel overdrive stage morphing from soft clip to two-stage wavefolder. DC-coupled.

controls.name: Resonance
controls.description: Emphasis around cutoff. Reaches clean self-oscillation with constant amplitude.

controls.name: Drive
controls.description: Gain and distortion amount feeding the filter core. Also acts as the offset for the DRIVE CV input.

controls.name: Drive Shape
controls.description: Morphs the drive circuit from round soft-clipping to a two-stage wavefolder.

controls.name: Response
controls.description: Continuously morphs between low-pass, band-pass, and high-pass. Intermediate settings have a shelving shape useful for EQ.
controls.detail: when self-oscillating, adjusts the phase of the sine output

controls.name: Routing
controls.description: Dual crossfader from single (filter 1 only) through parallel (mix of both) to series (filter 1 into filter 2).
controls.detail: CCW single · 12 o'clock parallel · CW series

controls.name: CV Attenuverters
controls.description: Bipolar attenuverters for filter 1 and 2 frequency and resonance CV inputs, plus the routing CV input.

controls.name: Cutoff Coupling
controls.description: Button/LED. When on, filter 2's frequency knob sets an offset relative to filter 1's cutoff; at 12 o'clock both filters share the same cutoff.

inputs.name: Mode CV
inputs.description: CV over filter response (LP/BP/HP morph). When a filter self-oscillates this acts as phase modulation of the sine output.

inputs.name: Cutoff CV
inputs.description: Attenuverted CV over cutoff frequency, per filter.

inputs.name: Resonance CV
inputs.description: Attenuverted CV over resonance, per filter.

inputs.name: Routing CV
inputs.description: Attenuverted CV over the single / parallel / series routing between the two filters.

inputs.name: V/OCT
inputs.description: Unattenuated frequency CV, calibrated for 1V/octave tracking of cutoff (and of the self-oscillation pitch).

inputs.name: Drive CV
inputs.description: CV over drive amount. Normalized to +2 V when unpatched, which is why a moderate gain is obtained with DRIVE fully CCW.

inputs.name: Filter 1 IN
inputs.description: DC-coupled signal input to filter 1. Accepts audio or CV (the path is DC-coupled so portamento/slew on CV is possible).

inputs.name: Filter 2 IN
inputs.description: DC-coupled signal input to filter 2. When left unpatched, filter 2 receives a blend of filter 1's input and output set by the ROUTING control (this is what creates the single/parallel/series morph). Patching a signal here breaks the internal routing and turns ROUTING into a crossfader between the two filters.

outputs.name: Main Output
outputs.description: Mix of filter 1 and filter 2, balanced by the ROUTING control and CV: only filter 1 at CCW, 50/50 at noon, only filter 2 at CW.

behaviors.name: Single / parallel / series routing
behaviors.description: ROUTING is a dual crossfader. It sets how much of filter 1 vs filter 2 reaches MAIN out, and also how much of filter 1's input vs filter 1's output feeds filter 2 (when Filter 2 IN is unpatched). CCW = filter 1 alone, noon = both in parallel on the same signal, CW = filter 1 feeding filter 2 in series.

behaviors.name: Patch filter 2 for crossfader
behaviors.trigger: patch a signal into Filter 2 IN
behaviors.description: Patching Filter 2 IN overrides the internal routing. ROUTING then behaves as a simple crossfader between the two independent filters at the MAIN output.

behaviors.name: Cutoff coupling
behaviors.description: When engaged, filter 2's FREQUENCY knob sets a relative offset to filter 1's cutoff. Noon = same cutoff; past/before noon transposes filter 2 up/down while filter 1 drives the pair.

behaviors.name: Clean self-oscillation / sine VCO
behaviors.description: Both filters self-oscillate cleanly with constant amplitude across the audio range and track 1V/oct via V/OCT. Each filter becomes a usable sine oscillator.

behaviors.name: Response as phase on oscillation
behaviors.description: When a filter is self-oscillating, modulating its Response (via knob or Mode CV) shifts the phase of the sine output. Audio-rate Mode CV gives FM-like metallic tones; a VCA on Mode CV gives CV-controlled PM depth.

behaviors.name: Drive morphs clip to fold
behaviors.description: The pre-filter drive stage morphs continuously from soft clipping to a two-stage wavefolder. Combined with DRIVE level and Drive CV it becomes a CV-controlled overdrive/folder ahead of each filter.

behaviors.name: Filter-response shelving
behaviors.trigger: Response knob between LP/BP or BP/HP
behaviors.description: Intermediate Response positions produce shelving shapes rather than pure LP/BP/HP, which makes Blades effective as a spectral shaper or tone control.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled CV processing
behaviors.trigger: feed CV into a filter input
behaviors.description: Because the signal path is DC-coupled, Blades can process CV: FREQUENCY as portamento, RESONANCE to add overshoot, DRIVE to scale or shape control voltages.

patchIdeas.name: Series drive into low-pass
patchIdeas.patch: Patch a saw VCO into Filter 1 IN. Set ROUTING fully clockwise for series. Both filters on low-pass, filter 1 with moderate DRIVE around 11 o'clock, filter 2 clean and resonant. MAIN out to a VCA. You get grit shaped into filter 2's sweep.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* Routing: series (CW)
* Filter 1: LP, Drive 11 o'clock, Resonance low
* Filter 2: LP, Resonance medium-high

VCO:
Envelope:
VCA:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Blades (Filter 1 IN)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Envelope (Out) >> Blades (Cutoff CV)
- Blades (Main Output) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Parallel split-band or stereo
patchIdeas.patch: ROUTING at 12 o'clock for parallel. Cutoff Coupling off. Filter 1 set to LP, filter 2 set to HP, both with the same source at Filter 1 IN. Take Filter 1 Out and Filter 2 Out to L/R for a wide split-band processor.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* Routing: parallel (noon)
* Cutoff Coupling: off
* Filter 1: LP, Cutoff low-mid
* Filter 2: HP, Cutoff mid-high

VCO:
LFO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Blades (Filter 1 IN)
- LFO (Out) >> Blades (Cutoff CV, filter 1)
- Blades (Filter 1 Output) -> Output (L)
- Blades (Filter 2 Output) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Dual sine VCO with coupling
patchIdeas.patch: Both Resonance knobs at max for self-oscillation. Cutoff Coupling on. Set filter 2 FREQUENCY above noon for a fifth offset. V/OCT tracks the pair. Use Filter 1 Out and Filter 2 Out as two sine voices.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* No input patched
* Resonance 1 and 2: max (self-osc)
* Cutoff Coupling: on
* Filter 2 Frequency: ~3 o'clock (fifth up)

Sequencer:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Blades (V/OCT)
- Blades (Filter 1 Output) -> Output (L)
- Blades (Filter 2 Output) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Phase-modulated metallic voice
patchIdeas.patch: Filter 1 self-oscillating (Res max, no audio in). V/OCT tracks pitch. A second VCO (or LFO at audio rate) through a VCA into Mode CV 1 gives CV-controlled phase modulation, FM-like metallic timbres from Filter 1 Out.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* Filter 1: Resonance max, Response knob noon
* No signal at Filter 1 IN

VCO Carrier Pitch:
VCO Modulator:
VCA:
Envelope:
Output:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Blades (V/OCT)
- VCO Modulator (Sine) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) >> Blades (Mode CV filter 1)
- Blades (Filter 1 Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: CV-controlled wavefolder
patchIdeas.patch: Use Blades as a folder ahead of an external filter: feed VCO into Filter 1 IN, set Drive Shape fully CW (fold), Resonance low, Response low-pass, modulate Drive CV with an envelope. Take Filter 1 Out post-fold-and-LP.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* Filter 1: LP, Resonance low, Drive Shape = fold
* Routing: single (CCW)

VCO:
Envelope:
Output:

- VCO (Sine) -> Blades (Filter 1 IN)
- Envelope (Out) >> Blades (Drive CV)
- Blades (Filter 1 Output) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Cross-patched chaos
patchIdeas.patch: Patch Filter 2 Out to Filter 1 Cutoff CV and Filter 1 Out to Filter 2 Cutoff CV with moderate attenuverter settings. Feed a sustained tone into Filter 1 IN, ROUTING at noon, and drive up Resonance on both. Expect chaotic timbres.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Blades:
* Routing: parallel (noon)
* Resonance 1 and 2: medium-high

VCO:
Output:

- VCO (Saw) -> Blades (Filter 1 IN)
- Blades (Filter 2 Output) >> Blades (Cutoff CV filter 1)
- Blades (Filter 1 Output) >> Blades (Cutoff CV filter 2)
- Blades (Main Output) -> Output (In)
